title: rosa canina f. kesmarkensis
intro: Rosa canina f. kesmarkensis, also known as the Kesmark rose, is a subspecies of the dog rose (Rosa canina) native to the Carpathian Mountains region. This shrub is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Rosa canina f. kesmarkensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or using the dried flowers to make capsules or tinctures.
